content:
type: DEFER
files: Tests/test_image_quantize.py
spec: Please add tests for the new error_threshold parameter on Image.quantize(). Spec: quantize(self, colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, error_threshold=None) gains a new optional keyword-only-by-convention arg 'error_threshold' (float, default None = disabled). When error_threshold is not None: after generating a quantized palette/image, compute the mean squared error (MSE) between the original RGB image and the quantized+depaletted image; if using colors <= requested 'colors' already meets MSE <= error_threshold, use the smallest color count (searched from low to 'colors') that first satisfies MSE <= error_threshold; otherwise fall back to using the full 'colors' count. Suggested tests to add in Tests/test_image_quantize.py: (1) test that calling im.quantize(colors=256, error_threshold=100) returns a 'P' mode image and the resulting image, converted back to RGB, has MSE against the original <= a generous bound OR uses <= 256 colors (len(im.getcolors()) <= 256); (2) test that a low error_threshold (e.g. error_threshold=1) on a simple/flat-color test image (e.g. Tests/images/caption_6_33_22.png or hopper.jpg) results in fewer colors used than a high threshold; (3) test that error_threshold=None (default) preserves existing behavior identical to calling quantize() without the parameter (same output palette/pixels as before); (4) test invalid values (e.g. negative error_threshold) raise ValueError. Use hopper() test image (from Tests.helper import hopper) as usual. I will implement the corresponding logic in src/PIL/Image.py quantize().

Use hopper() test image (from Tests.helper import hopper) as usual. I will implement the corresponding logic in src/PIL/Image.py quantize().

content:
type: SPEC
files: Tests/test_image_quantize.py
spec: error_threshold SPEC (resend, in case earlier message didn't arrive): quantize() gains keyword arg error_threshold=None (a float, default None = disabled), placed after sort_palette in the signature I own: quantize(self, colors=256, method=None, kmeans=0, palette=None, dither=1, sort_palette=None, error_threshold=None). Behavior: only applies in the non-palette-reference branch. If error_threshold is not None: validate it is a non-negative number, else raise ValueError('error_threshold must be a non-negative number'). When set, quantize repeatedly with color counts (e.g. starting small, doubling or stepping up to 'colors') computing MSE between original RGB pixels and the quantized image converted back to RGB; use the SMALLEST color count <= 'colors' whose MSE <= error_threshold; if none meets the threshold even at 'colors', just use 'colors' colors (current default behavior). error_threshold=None must be 100% behavior-preserving (identical output to current code). Please add these tests to Tests/test_image_quantize.py: (1) def test_error_threshold_reduces_colors(self): open hopper('RGB'), call im.quantize(colors=256, error_threshold=500), assert result.mode == 'P', assert len(result.getcolors()) <= 256; also assert a high/loose error_threshold (e.g. 10000) on a simple flat-color image (Image.new('RGB', (100,100), 'red')) yields fewer colors used than the same image quantized with error_threshold=0 or a very strict threshold. (2) def test_error_threshold_none_matches_default(self): assert hopper().quantize(colors=64).tobytes() == hopper().quantize(colors=64, error_threshold=None).tobytes() (default must be unchanged). (3) def test_error_threshold_invalid(self): assert_raises ValueError for hopper().quantize(error_threshold=-1). Use  as already imported in that file, and place new tests in the existing TestImageQuantize class.
note: final error_threshold test spec for you to implement
